ogTitle: Swansea vs Swansea Heads: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Swansea and Swansea Heads.
heading: Swansea vs Swansea Heads — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Swansea, New South Wales and Swansea Heads, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Swansea has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Swansea Heads (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Swansea or Swansea Heads safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Swansea has a flood risk score of 24/100, while Swansea Heads scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Swansea scores 25/100 for bushfire risk. Swansea Heads scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Swansea or Swansea Heads?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Swansea has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.15% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
